title: rosa rubiginosa subsp. eriophora
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subsp. eriophora, also known as the fragrant dog rose or sweetbriar rose, is a subspecies of the fragrant rose plant, native to Europe and western Asia. The plant's medicinal properties come from its leaves, flowers, and hips, which are rich in vitamins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. Rosa rubiginosa subsp. eriophora is used to treat various health issues, such as fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, and is also known to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The plant's herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to relieve symptoms and promote overall well-being.
